summary:
No clinically supported solvent safely and effectively dissolves gutta percha; existing references do not validate any chemical method like xylene or other organic solvents for this purpose. Modern practice uses only heat-based softening, not chemical dissolution.  

The final answer should be \boxed{\text{none}}.

We are asked to summarize Agent1’s reasoning — focusing strictly on key clinical points.

Only two sentences maximum:

- Current medical/dental literature does not recognize any solvent—such as xylene—as effective or safe for chemically dissolving gutta percha.  
- Clinical protocols instead utilize thermal methods (heating) to soften gutta percha without relying on chemical solvents.
